description:
You are acting as an on-call Linux support engineer who has been asked to gather a concise, machine-readable snapshot of basic system resources.  
    Three already-collected diagnostic text files are waiting for you under the directory  
        /home/user/diagnostics  

    1. /home/user/diagnostics/cpu_dump.txt  
    2. /home/user/diagnostics/mem_dump.txt  
    3. /home/user/diagnostics/disk_dump.txt  

    Each of these files contains exactly three lines.  
      • Every line follows the fixed pattern “Key: Value” (a literal colon followed by a single space).  
      • Keys and values themselves never contain a colon.  
      • No blank lines or trailing spaces exist in the source files.

    Your assignment is to extract only the value portion (everything to the right of “: ”) from every line in the three files, then combine those values into a single tab-separated matrix file.

    OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS
    -------------------
    1. Create the directory /home/user/output if it does not already exist.  
    2. Create the file  /home/user/output/diag_matrix.tsv   with the following properties:

       • Exactly 3 rows and 3 columns.  
       • A single tab character (“\t”) separates each column.  
       • No spaces should precede or follow a tab character.  
       • Lines are terminated with the Unix newline character (“\n”).  
       • Row order MUST be:  
            a. all values from cpu_dump.txt (in the same order they appear there)  
            b. all values from mem_dump.txt (same order)  
            c. all values from disk_dump.txt (same order)  
       • There must be NO header row, NO key names, and NO extra blank line at the end of the file.

       Visually, when tabs are shown as “⟶”, the final file must look exactly like this:

       Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2630 v4 @ 2.20GHz⟶16⟶2
       32000 MB⟶12000 MB⟶15000 MB
       500 GB⟶123 GB⟶377 GB

       (Do not literally include the “⟶” symbols; they represent single tab characters.)

    3. While working, keep an audit trail of every command you execute (one command per line, no extra commentary).  
       Append each command, in chronological order, to  
           /home/user/output/commands.log  

       Example expected appearance (yours will differ, but must follow the same “one command per line” rule):

           cut -d':' -f2 /home/user/diagnostics/cpu_dump.txt > cpu_vals.tmp
           paste ...

       The commands.log file may contain additional commands used for verification or mistakes—as long as every command you physically ran on the shell appears and each entry is separated by a single newline.

    4. After creating diag_matrix.tsv, display its content to the terminal so a human reviewer can visually confirm success.

    TOOL CONSTRAINT
    ---------------
    You may use any standard POSIX utilities present on a typical Debian/Ubuntu installation (e.g., cut, paste, tr, awk, sed, cat, echo, etc.). Root access is not available, so restrict yourself to user-land commands.

    COMPLEXITY TARGET
    -----------------
    The entire job can reasonably be completed with 5–10 distinct shell commands (not counting commands used only to look around with ls or cat).

    VERIFICATION
    ------------
    Automated tests will confirm all of the following:

      • /home/user/output/diag_matrix.tsv exists and its byte-for-byte contents match the specification above.  
      • /home/user/output/commands.log exists and contains at least 5 newline-separated, non-empty lines.  
      • Ownership and permissions are such that the regular user can read both files.

    When you have finished, ensure that the last thing printed to STDOUT by your session is the full, correct content of /home/user/output/diag_matrix.tsv.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing files placed before the task begins
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/cpu_dump.txt"><![CDATA[
Model name: Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2630 v4 @ 2.20GHz
CPU(s): 16
Thread(s) per core: 2
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/mem_dump.txt"><![CDATA[
MemTotal: 32000 MB
MemFree: 12000 MB
MemAvailable: 15000 MB
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/diagnostics/disk_dump.txt"><![CDATA[
DiskSize: 500 GB
DiskUsed: 123 GB
DiskAvailable: 377 GB
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        Expected artefacts after successful task completion
    -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/output/diag_matrix.tsv"><![CDATA[
Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2630 v4 @ 2.20GHz	16	2
32000 MB	12000 MB	15000 MB
500 GB	123 GB	377 GB
]]></file>
    </expected_files>

    <!--
        commands.log must exist and have >=5 newline-separated commands;
        exact content is not mandated, only that each line is non-empty text.
    -->
